title: BBP/Bellard hex-digit extractor (independent code path)
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. The BBP/Bellard hex extractor is a genuinely independent code path used as a verification cross-check against Method A; its independence is what makes the spot-checks meaningful. It depends on the arch-001 ADRs.
objective: Implement a BBP/Bellard hex-digit extractor that computes hex digits of pi at an arbitrary offset via an algorithmically independent path from the Chudnovsky implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Implements BBP/Bellard hex-digit extraction at an arbitrary digit offset as a code path genuinely independent of the Method A (Chudnovsky) implementation.
Extracted hex digits match a known reference value for spot-checked offsets.
Deterministic: identical input produces identical output on repeat runs.
TypeScript/ESM, builds via pnpm; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; MIT-licensed.
output: A PR adding the BBP/Bellard hex-digit extractor as an independent verification code path.
